observations: The table itself renders no images. `header`/`rows`/`footer` are trusted HTML strings the implementer supplies (see `CtTable`'s class doc comment), so any image/icon embedded in cell content needs its own text alternative supplied by the implementer - this component cannot enforce that.

standard: 1.2.3 Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded) (Level A)

observations: The single-row column-header case is handled automatically: any non-empty `header` is always wrapped in a real `<thead><tr>` and each cell the implementer supplies is expected to be a `<th scope="col">` (documented in the `header` prop's JSDoc/schema description), giving assistive technology the header/data-cell relationship for free. What the component cannot supply on its own: row headers (`scope="row"` on a `<th>` within a body row) or multi-row header structures (`colspan`/`headers`/`id` references for complex tables) - those require the implementer to author them directly into the `rows` HTML string.

DOM order is always header, then body rows, then footer, matching the visual reading order; the sub-768px stacked layout re-flows each row into a single flex column without reordering its cells, so linearized (e.g. screen-reader or reflow) reading order matches the visual one at every viewport width.
The component imposes no orientation lock and reflows identically in portrait or landscape.
The table renders no form inputs of its own.

The `striped` modifier's alternating row background/text colors are a decorative readability aid layered on top of the borders and header/body typography contrast that already exist in the unstriped default - no information is conveyed by the striping alone that isn't otherwise available from structure and text.

Computed against the component's own `table.*` design tokens: light theme body text (#33444a) against its lightest row background (#fdfdfd) is 9.99:1, and against the alternating row background (#e6e9eb) is 8.33:1; light theme header/footer/caption text (#002a39) against #fdfdfd is 14.85:1. Dark theme body text (#e7f9ff) against its background (#003a4f) is 11.29:1 and against the alternating row background (#0d4458) is 9.77:1; dark theme header/footer/caption text (#f7fdff) against #003a4f is 11.9:1. All pairs clear the 4.5:1 AA minimum for normal text with substantial margin.
All typography in `static styles` is set in `rem`/token units, with no fixed-pixel font sizes, so text is still visible and readable when resized to at least 200%.
Compliance depends on the implementer avoiding images of text within the trusted `header`/`rows`/`footer` HTML unless essential (e.g. a logo).

Automatic for the default mode: below 768px a non-`data-table` restructures into a single-column stacked/card layout, so it never requires two-dimensional scrolling at a 320 CSS px viewport. The `data-table` variant deliberately opts out of that restructuring to preserve a conventional grid for dense tabular data (matching upstream's `is_data_table`) - a wide `data-table` can need horizontal scrolling at narrow widths, and (matching upstream, which also does not apply this automatically) the implementer is responsible for wrapping it in a horizontally-scrollable container to stay compliant.
Row/header/footer/caption borders are conventional structural dividers between rows of the same table, not UI-component boundaries this criterion requires to meet 3:1 contrast.
Text is visible/readable when the line spacing is at least 1.5 times the font size, letter spacing is at least 0.12 times the font size, and word spacing is at least 0.16 times the font size.

standard: 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus (Level AA)

observations: The component itself renders no interactive elements. Any interactive content (links, buttons) an implementer embeds in the trusted `header`/`rows`/`footer` HTML remains ordinary HTML rendered inside this component's own shadow root (not slotted), so it keeps normal keyboard operability without any interference from `ct-table`.
Static markup; nothing in `ct-table` captures or withholds focus.

standard: 2.3.1 Three Flashes or Below Threshold (Level A)

observations: Only relevant when the implementer embeds focusable content (links/buttons) in cell HTML. DOM order is always header row, then body rows top-to-bottom/left-to-right, then footer row, which preserves a meaningful focus order for whatever focusable content is present - but the actual content and its order within a cell is the implementer's responsibility, not something `ct-table` generates.
Only relevant when the implementer's `header`/`rows`/`footer` HTML embeds links; compliance depends on those links having clear and descriptive text.

A non-empty `caption` gives the table a programmatically-associated accessible name via the native `<caption>` element, the recommended labelling mechanism for `<table>` - but `caption` is optional, and the individual `<th>` header text's clarity is entirely determined by whatever HTML the implementer supplies via `header`.

`static styles` sets no `:focus`/`:focus-visible` overrides that would suppress the browser default, so any focusable content an implementer embeds in cell HTML keeps its normal focus-visible treatment - but whether such content exists at all is the implementer's choice, not something this component provides.
The table's own accessible name (its `caption`, when provided) is plain visible text with no separate `aria-label` that could diverge from it.
The component defines no pointer targets of its own (cells are not clickable); the target size of any interactive content an implementer embeds in cell HTML is that content's own concern.
Compliance depends on the implementer setting the correct lang attribute on the html element.
Compliance depends on the implementer setting lang attributes on parts of the page (or within the trusted `rows`/`header`/`footer` HTML) that differ from the main language.
Nothing in `ct-table`'s own markup is focusable, so it cannot itself trigger an on-focus context change.
The table renders no form inputs of its own.
The table has no form validation or error states of its own.
The table has no form inputs requiring labels or instructions.

`ct-table` renders only native `<table>`/`<caption>`/`<thead>`/`<tbody>`/`<tfoot>`/`<tr>`/`<th scope="col">`/`<td>` markup, entirely generated by the component itself from its own props (not split across two separately-authored elements the way a `for`/`id` label association is) - the correct table/row/columnheader/cell accessibility roles are exposed by the browser automatically, with no additional implementer action required beyond supplying `caption`/`header` text.
The table is static once rendered; it has no dynamic state changes of its own to announce via a live region.
